asset_name: Echo Behavior Core Sheet — idle, eating, hungry, dirty
shared_properties: Front-facing cockatiel silhouette uses crest, cheek patch, beak, and tail in every state.
Body footprint stays inside 40x40px so scene composition stays stable.
All frames keep Echo as the most saturated focal element in the scene.
differences: This sheet covers care-critical readable states needed for the short core loop.
Rest, affection, and singing states stay in the companion character sheet.

reference_images.label: proxy_character_silhouette
reference_images.purpose: Proxy sprite reference for keeping a compact readable bird silhouette at 1x scale.

reference_images.label: proxy_pixel_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Proxy moodboard for warm pixel treatment and gentle animation density.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load assets/art/pico_echo/trial_5/character_echo_behavior_core_sheet_21f.png with AssetServer during Loading state and store the handle under character_echo_behavior_core_sheet_21f.
runtime_notes: Create a 6x4 TextureAtlasLayout and map state ranges exactly to the documented frame spans.
Advance frames with state-specific timers so idle plays at 2 fps and eating plays at 4 fps.
Keep the sprite anchored near x=44 y=40 inside the 128x128 room shell for every core state.

context.usage: Core loop sheet used during idle watch, feeding, hunger warning, and dirty discomfort reactions.

silhouette_notes: Crest rises 3px above the head in alert frames and stays visible in every state.
Cheek patch stays solid #FF004D at 4 to 6 pixels on the face.
Tail projects down-right by 6 to 8px and never clips the 40x40 frame.
Hungry frames angle the head 6 to 8px toward the feed bowl side.

shape.silhouette: 40x40 cockatiel with a 3-point crest, round chest, short beak, visible cheek patch, folded wings, and a tail angled 6px to 8px down-right.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 240x160px with six columns and four rows of 40x40 frames.
Frames 0 to 16 contain the four named states and frames 17 to 20 remain usable polish frames or transparent reserve frames documented in source.
Echo face remains readable at 1x scale with distinct eyes, crest, cheek patch, and beak in every occupied frame.
Only listed colors and transparency are used, with no anti-aliasing.
